question_title: Will H.R. 33 (United States–Taiwan Expedited Double-Tax Relief Act) be enacted into law before May 1, 2026?
summary: H.R. 33 passed the House 423-1 on Jan 15, 2025 but has seen zero Senate action in over 13 months. The companion bill S. 199 was introduced by bipartisan Senate committee chairs but also hasn't moved. The Taiwan tax provisions were not included in the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (the major reconciliation vehicle, signed July 4, 2025). The previous 118th Congress version also died without becoming law. With ~2 months until the May 1, 2026 deadline and no scheduled committee markup or floor action, enactment is very unlikely. The only realistic pathway is unanimous consent passage, which is theoretically possible given strong bipartisan support but has shown no signs of materializing after 13+ months.

factors.description: Bill has been sitting in Senate since Jan 16, 2025 - over 13 months with zero action (no committee markup, no floor scheduling)
Previous 118th Congress versions never became law despite Senate Finance Committee markup of S.3084 in Sept 2023 - pattern of this issue stalling
Senate Finance Committee has NOT held a markup on the 119th Congress version despite bipartisan introduction by committee chairs Crapo and Wyden
Only ~2 months remain until May 1, 2026 deadline - bill needs Senate passage and presidential signature in compressed window
Senate occupied with reconciliation aftermath, partial government shutdown, and other higher-priority items
Overwhelming bipartisan support (423-1 House vote, bipartisan Senate committee chairs) theoretically enables unanimous consent passage
Taiwan tax provisions NOT included in One Big Beautiful Bill Act (P.L. 119-21, signed July 4, 2025) - verified via IRS provisions listing, suggesting low Senate priority
November 2025 multi-association letter urging passage confirms bill still hasn't advanced despite continued advocacy

reasoning:
**Forecast: 5% probability** that H.R. 33 is enacted before May 1, 2026.

**Key reasoning:**
- The bill passed the House overwhelmingly (423-1) in January 2025 but has been completely stalled in the Senate for 13+ months
- No Senate Finance Committee markup, no floor scheduling, no attachment to the major reconciliation bill (OBBBA)
- The 118th Congress version followed a similar pattern of stalling
- Only ~2 months remain, with a packed Senate agenda
- The sole realistic pathway is unanimous consent, which is theoretically possible given bipartisan support from committee chairs but shows no signs of materializing

resolution_criteria: This question resolves Yes if, after the time the question opens and before May 1, 2026, Congress.gov shows H.R. 33 has become law (e.g., “Became Public Law No: …” or equivalent enacted status) on the bill’s official page: [https://www.congress.gov/bill/119th-congress/house-bill/33](https://www.congress.gov/bill/119th-congress/house-bill/33). If Congress.gov does not show enacted/public-law status by the resolution time, the question resolves No.
fine_print: “Enacted” requires becoming law and public-law designation, not merely House/Senate passage. If Congress.gov’s status is temporarily delayed, use the latest available Congress.gov status as of the resolution time; if still unclear, Candidate Sources are: GovInfo bill-to-law publications and public-law records ([https://www.govinfo.gov/app/collection/plaw](https://www.govinfo.gov/app/collection/plaw)) and the “All Actions” tab for H.R. 33 on Congress.gov ([https://www.congress.gov/bill/119th-congress/house-bill/33/all-actions](https://www.congress.gov/bill/119th-congress/house-bill/33/all-actions)).
background_info:
Enactment would materially change the tax treatment of qualifying Taiwan residents’ U.S.-source income and would be a concrete signal of deepening U.S.–Taiwan economic alignment.
